images.caption: Figure 1:Up-to-Date Leaderboard: Coding agent performance on SaaSBench evaluation tasks.

Figure 2:Overview of SaaSBench. The benchmark is grounded in real software development markets and constructs tasks through a multi-stage human-agent collaborative process. Evaluation is conducted with a reproducible dependency-aware hybrid evaluation paradigm.

Figure 3:Statistical overview of SaaSBench. Left: SaaSBench includes six key SaaS domains and 30 fine-grained categories, covering mainstream software development markets. Right: Distribution of tasks across programming languages, database types, and frontend and backend frameworks.

Table 2:Main results on SaaSBench across different agent–model configurations.
We report the overall Pass@1 and Node Coverage, together with scores over the six SaaS domains.
Within each coding agent block,boldnumbers indicate the best performance, andunderlinednumbers indicate the second-best performance.

Table 3:Engineering capability dimensions results on SaaSBench.
We report per-category scores for Deploy, Data, API, Logic, AuthZ, and Quality.

Figure 4:Left:Performance analysis of agent frameworks.Right:We classify capability units into five execution trajectories. T4 and T5
account for 95.6% of all units, showing that most failures occur before agents
reach deep business logic. See AppendixB.7for
definitions.
